description:
**fz-dps.CD**: native-path wire ABI (Return/Call/TailCall) + MakeList DPS via TRMC.

Combines C2 (Term::Return), C3 (Term::Call/CallClosure), C4 (Term::TailCall), D2' (MakeList DPS via TRMC), and E2 (drop uniform emit_return for Dst specs). Cannot land atomically as separate commits — wire ABI without a constructor is a regression, and at least one constructor must be DPS-aware for the wire change to demonstrate any savings.

**Why MakeList first**: cons cells are 2 words, trivial constructor surface. The recursive cons-in-result pattern `[f(h) | map(f, t)]` is the canonical TRMC target. `fixtures/list_primitives` already classifies `@dps List` on map/reverse/reverse_acc.

**Scope**:

1. **C2 — Term::Return under Dst**: when `return_reprs[my_sid] = Dst(k)`, lower `Return(v)` to `store v through dst; return`. Cont sig becomes `(dst_i64) -> ()`.

2. **C3 — Term::Call/CallClosure under Dst**: when callee's return-repr is `Dst(k)` *and* caller has an eligible dst slot, pass dst as extra arg; skip return-path materialisation. **Eligibility lookup rides `Module.callsite_outcomes` (fz-9pr/fz-f88)** — `Emitted { target: (fn_id, descrs) }` resolves the callsite to a spec whose `return_reprs[sid]` is the source of truth. No bespoke walk; if there's no `Emitted` entry the callsite stays uniform.

3. **C4 — Term::TailCall/TailCallClosure**: forward caller's dst unchanged to tail callee. For TRMC-transformed sites, forward `&cell.tail` of newly-bumped cell as callee dst.

4. **D2' — MakeList DPS via TRMC**: when surrounding cont's dst is List and MakeList appears in result position with one tail-recursive call: alloc cons cell, write head, transform recursive call to tail-call with `&cell.tail` as dst. **Base case (`[]` in result position): write the empty-list sentinel (TAG_PTR | 1, bit pattern `1`) through dst — NOT `NIL_BITS`.** Post-fz-s9y the empty list and `nil` are distinct values (runtime/src/fz_value.rs:43-46).

5. **E2 cleanup**: emit_return helper no longer used for Dst specs; drop its always-tag path.

**Tag-scheme note**: as-shipped fz-o0u uses TAG_PTR=0b000 (not the 0b001 the original ticket text assumed). Heap derefs through tagged pointers need no `untag_ptr`; q9g.24's audit confirmed clean on all post-baseline load/store sites.

**Proof fixture**: `fixtures/list_primitives` — rebless `expected.clif`. Expected delta: `reverse_acc` and `map`'s cons specs gain `dst` parameter, lose return-path FzValue tag/untag, gain inline cell-bump + tail-call-with-dst. CLIF instruction count strictly decreases on those specs.

**Tests**: rebless list_primitives golden CLIF; all fixture-matrix paths green; clippy clean; warnings = errors.

**Out of scope** (separate tickets): F0=VecI64, F1=VecF64/U8/Bit, F2=Bitstring, F3=Tuple, F4-literal=known-length list literals, D1=case-arm shared dst (depends on this).

comments.text: Scope refined per user: native-path only. Uniform-path DPS deferred to fz-ag9 (CD.2).

Rewritten 2026-05-18 to (a) name Module.callsite_outcomes (fz-9pr/fz-f88) as the eligibility source for C3, (b) correct MakeList base case to empty-list sentinel (bit pattern 1) rather than NIL_BITS — post fz-s9y/fz-yan [] and nil are distinct, (c) note that the as-shipped fz-o0u (TAG_PTR=0b000) makes the q9g.24 untag concern moot.

title: fz-dps.CD: native-path wire ABI (Return/Call/TailCall) + MakeList DPS via TRMC
